messages.content:
Which process of the enterprise platform interacts with vendors and manages the entire supply chain for a retail client?


Data


Experience


Core Processing


Front Office


I don't know this yet.
The process of the enterprise platform that interacts with vendors and manages the entire supply chain for a retail client is the Core Processing process.

When evaluating platforms, what is the key factor in ensuring a platform addresses an organization’s needs?


choosing the platform that can support the most users simultaneously


choosing the platform with the lowest cost of implementation


using technical documentation for similar legacy applications as a reference


mapping the platform against defined business objectives


I don't know this yet.

When evaluating platforms, the key factor in ensuring a platform addresses an organization's needs is mapping the platform against defined business objectives. This involves aligning the features, capabilities, and functionalities of the platform with the specific goals and requirements of the organization.

A new Accenture client is looking for a platform that will help them in hiring for critical roles and decreasing attrition. Which Accenture tool will help the client select the best type of platform for overcoming these challenges?


myIndustry


DevOps


myConcerto


SynOps


I don't know this yet.
The Accenture tool that will help the client select the best type of platform for hiring critical roles and decreasing attrition is myConcerto.

Which statement describes an enterprise platform?


It is a technology that directly connects a supplier with the consumer to complete a task or achieve a goal.


It is a platform that directly connects a supplier with a consumer through a social networking interface to complete end-to-end business.


It is a framework that connects people, processes, and other technologies together to deliver on a strategic business goal.


It is a platform that helps people connect and share content with colleagues and friends for social and business purposes.


I don't know this yet.

The statement that describes an enterprise platform is: "It is a framework that connects people, processes, and other technologies together to deliver on a strategic business goal." An enterprise platform typically integrates various resources, systems, and individuals within an organization to enable efficient collaboration and achieve specific business objectives.

What is the core function of an enterprise platform?


It manages the primary functions of a business.


It helps employees interact with their supervisors.


It connects the supplier with the consumer.


It hosts all businesses and other platforms.


I don't know this yet.

The core function of an enterprise platform is to manage the primary functions of a business. It provides tools, processes, and resources to support various business operations, such as financial management, supply chain management, human resources, customer relationship management, and more.

Which describes the relationship between enterprise platforms and the cloud?


Data on the cloud can be analyzed and monitored without the need for a platform.


All enterprise platforms are cloud-based.


Enterprise platforms are an alternative to hosting solutions on the cloud.


Enterprise platforms are primarily built around and hosted on the cloud.


I don't know this yet.

The statement that best describes the relationship between enterprise platforms and the cloud is: "Enterprise platforms are primarily built around and hosted on the cloud." Many enterprise platforms are designed to leverage the cloud infrastructure for hosting, scalability, and accessibility purposes. However, it is important to note that not all enterprise platforms are exclusively cloud-based, as some can be hosted on-premises or in hybrid environments as well.

How can migrating to an enterprise platform solution help a business improve employee productivity?


by allowing all employees to access and share the same stored data faster


by adding complexity to each employee’s daily workload


by freeing up employee’s time to focus on core business activities


by increasing the number of employees in a business function


I don't know this yet.

Migrating to an enterprise platform solution can help a business improve employee productivity by freeing up employee's time to focus on core business activities. By automating repetitive tasks, streamlining processes, and centralizing data, an enterprise platform allows employees to spend less time on manual and administrative tasks, ultimately enabling them to dedicate more time and effort to strategic and value-added activities. This can lead to increased productivity and efficiency within the organization.

A major coffee retailer is looking to implement an enterprise platform. In particular, the company is looking to improve its supply chain management.

Which component of an enterprise platform focuses on this aspect of the company’s needs?


Collaborative


Data


Core Processing


Experience


I don't know this yet.

The component of an enterprise platform that focuses on supply chain management and would address the needs of a coffee retailer is the Collaborative component. This component enables effective communication, coordination, and collaboration between various stakeholders in the supply chain, including suppliers, distributors, logistics providers, and retailers. It helps optimize inventory management, track shipments, streamline procurement processes, and ensure smooth interaction throughout the entire supply chain.

How can an organization customize and extend the functionality of a platform?


By requesting the platform vendor to share the source code of a product so it can be customized on-premise by local developers.


By interacting with its APIs to develop extensions and tailor existing functionalities to the needs of the organization.


By customizing the platform's auto-configuration script according to the needs of the organization.


By interacting with its remote procedure calls (RPCs) to develop extensions and tailor existing functionalities to the needs of the organization.


I don't know this yet.

An organization can customize and extend the functionality of a platform by interacting with its APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) to develop extensions and tailor existing functionalities to the needs of the organization. APIs provide a set of predefined methods and protocols that allow developers to interact with the platform and access its features and data. By leveraging these APIs, organizations can create custom integrations, develop additional functionality, and enhance the platform's capabilities to suit their specific requirements.

A company is acquiring a smaller firm and is setting up a new IT system in order to consolidate the data and assets of the acquisition.

When evaluating enterprise platforms for the new company, what is the primary aspect to consider?


size of the dataset to be reconciled


integration of the legacy platforms


the number of platforms needed


the location/capacity of the datacenters


I don't know this yet.

When evaluating enterprise platforms for setting up a new IT system to consolidate the data and assets of an acquisition, the primary aspect to consider is the integration of the legacy platforms. This involves assessing the compatibility and interoperability of the existing systems of both the acquiring company and the acquired company. Seamless integration of data, processes, and applications is crucial to ensure a smooth transition and consolidation of resources.

What is an example of work performed by an Integration Platform as a Service (IPaaS)?


handling administration of a company’s payroll and benefits


setting up and configuring cloud-based servers


transferring data between multiple third-party systems


connecting buyers and sellers in a virtual marketplace


I don't know this yet.

An example of work performed by an Integration Platform as a Service (iPaaS) is transferring data between multiple third-party systems. iPaaS solutions are designed to provide seamless integration and connectivity between various cloud-based and on-premises applications and systems. They facilitate the movement of data and enable smooth communication between different platforms, making it easier to transfer and synchronize information between multiple systems, such as customer relationship management (CRM) software, enterprise resource planning (ERP) systems, and more.

An online retailer is looking to implement an enterprise platform. Which component of the enterprise platform will help the company capture, curate, and consume customer information to improve their services?


Data and Insights


Experience


Core Process


Values and Principles


I don't know this yet.

The component of the enterprise platform that will help the online retailer capture, curate, and consume customer information to improve their services is the Data and Insights component. This component focuses on managing and analyzing customer data to gain valuable insights and make data-driven decisions. It includes capabilities for data collection, storage, processing, analytics, and visualization to understand customer behavior, preferences, and trends. By leveraging these insights, the retailer can enhance their services and tailor their offerings to meet customer needs more effectively.

Which areas of a business would most benefit from using the Workday platform?


private equity issuance and listing on a public exchange


human resources, compliance, recruiting, and finance


enterprise resource planning, supply chain, and inventory management


compliance, server machine administration, and anti-money laundering


I don't know this yet.

The areas of a business that would most benefit from using the Workday platform are human resources, compliance, recruiting, and finance. Workday is primarily known for its enterprise-level software solutions that focus on human capital management (HCM), including HR, recruiting, talent management, payroll, and finance functions. It streamlines HR and finance processes, enhances compliance and regulatory reporting, and provides tools for recruiting and talent management. Therefore, these specific areas of a business would experience the greatest benefits from implementing the Workday platform.

What differentiates Accenture’s Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) when our clients are making a decision to partner with a services company?


an ability to use pre-packaged software solutions from platform partners right out of the box


an ability to integrate deep technical knowledge, functional context, and industry experience


an ability to automate the product lifecycle for improved time to market and customer experience


an ability to create custom standalone applications and software specific to the business


I don't know this yet.

What differentiates Accenture's Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) when clients are making a decision to partner with a services company is the ability to integrate deep technical knowledge, functional context, and industry experience. Accenture's IPS focuses on leveraging its expertise in various technologies, combining it with a deep understanding of the client's industry and specific business requirements. This allows Accenture to offer tailored solutions that align with the client's goals and challenges, providing valuable insights and expertise throughout the project lifecycle. The emphasis on technical knowledge, functional context, and industry experience sets Accenture's IPS apart in the market.

An e-commerce client is moving from on-premise, legacy systems to a cloud-based platform. During the transition, the client is able to decommission several servers and applications, limiting their overall technology footprint.

How did this client benefit from using an enterprise platform?


by reducing cost and increasing the client’s growth opportunities with the same resources


by eliminating the need for process automation and testing


by instantly improving the quality of the client’s products


by significantly reducing the client’s need for human resources


I don't know this yet.

The client benefited from using an enterprise platform in this scenario by reducing costs and increasing growth opportunities with the same resources. By transitioning from on-premise, legacy systems to a cloud-based platform, the client was able to decommission several servers and applications. This resulted in cost savings by eliminating the need for maintaining and upgrading those infrastructure components. Additionally, the cloud-based platform provides scalability and flexibility, allowing the client to scale their operations and expand their business without the limitations of physical infrastructure. This enables the client to optimize resource utilization and allocate their resources more efficiently, leading to increased growth opportunities.
